Mario.overview: Well-rounded, great for learning fundamentals. No bad matchups, no dominant ones either.

skills: Get comfortable with short hopping (tap jump or tap the stick lightly)
Use N-air as your main approach — it's fast and safe
Learn F-smash for punishing laggy moves on the ground
Practice Up-tilt as an anti-air against opponents jumping at you
Use Cape (side-B) to reflect projectiles
D-throw into Up-air at low percentages (0-40%)
D-throw into Up-smash at mid percentages (40-80%) when opponent doesn't DI
Short hop fast fall (SHFF) N-air for pressure and shield pokes
Use Fireball to force approaches and control space
Learn B-reversing Fireball to move backward while shooting forward
Extended combo: D-throw → Up-tilt → Up-air → Up-air at 0-30%
Edge-cancel Fireballs off platforms for unpredictable angles
Use Cape off-stage to gimp recoveries — spike with Down-air below ledge
Shield-grab punishes after baiting attacks in neutral
Mix up throw follow-ups based on opponent's DI direction
Full neutral flowchart: Fireball → dash-in grab or F-tilt depending on reaction
Optimize punish game: always confirm the correct combo based on % and DI
Ledge trap setups with F-smash, dash attack, and grab reads
Two-framing opponents on the ledge with Down-tilt
Consistent off-stage play: when to spike, when to cape, when to retreat

Pikachu.overview: Small, fast, and combo-heavy. Strong edgeguarding. Requires precise inputs.

skills: Get used to Pikachu's small hurtbox — use it to avoid moves
Thunder Jolt (neutral-B) for controlling ground space
Quick Attack (up-B) for recovery — aim both hits carefully
N-air is your best landing option and combo starter
B-air for edgeguarding and ending stocks
D-throw into Up-smash at low percent
D-throw into Up-air chains at 0-50%
Thunder (down-B) to cover opponents above you or landing
Dash attack into Up-smash as a burst option
Use F-air to pressure shields and cover landings
Up-throw into Thunder for kills on floaties at high percent
Quick Attack cancel (QAC) for movement mixups and shield pressure
Extended juggle chains: Up-air → Up-air → Thunder to close stocks
Edgeguard with B-air spike or Thunder Jolt just off ledge
Wavebounce Thunder Jolts for pressure from both directions
QAC into grab or attack as a true mixup in neutral
Stock-ending flowchart: track DI on every throw to confirm the kill move
Gimp recoveries with Thunder Jolt timing off-stage
Perfect ledge trap setups using Thunder and dash attack coverage
Manage rage and weight interactions for optimized kill confirms

Joker.overview: High skill ceiling with Arsene mechanic. Weak without it, dominant with it.

skills: Learn Joker's movement — he has one of the best dash dances
N-air and B-air are your safest aerial tools
Gun (neutral-B) to poke and build Arsene meter
Grappling Hook (up-B) has two hitboxes — use it on-stage to attack
Understand the Arsene mechanic: it activates after taking ~100% damage
Up-throw into Up-air combo at low percent
F-air strings across the stage for carrying opponents
With Arsene: Eiha (down-B) plants a damage-over-time debuff — use it in neutral
With Arsene: Wings of Rebellion (up-B) kills early with the sweetspot
Practice managing and extending Arsene meter duration
Without Arsene: play patient — poke with Gun, bait with dash dance
With Arsene: play aggressive — all kill moves get massive buffs
Rebel's Guard (down-B without Arsene) absorbs hits and builds meter fast
Confirm kills with Up-smash and Back-throw near ledge with Arsene
Use Tetrakarn / Makarakarn (counters with Arsene) to hard-punish unsafe moves
Full dual-mode gameplan: safe neutral without Arsene, oppressive with it
Deliberately take hits with Rebel's Guard to activate Arsene on your terms
Edgeguard with Eiha + B-air to cover all recovery options off-stage
Dash dance spacing to bait attacks into maximum punishes
Kill confirm flowchart: percent ranges for Up-throw → Up-air vs Back-throw

Steve.overview: Unique resource mechanic with blocks. Dominant in the current meta but has a steep learning curve.

skills: Understand the mining mechanic — stand on ore deposits to gather materials
Place blocks (down-B) to extend combos and block approaches
Minecart (side-B) for pressure and approach coverage
N-air and F-air are your best aerial tools
Axe (F-smash) is a powerful but slow kill move — use it to punish hard
Block extension combos: Up-tilt → block → Up-tilt for juggles
TNT (down-B at full resources) for stage control and zone lockdown
Use Minecart offstage to gimp recoveries
Manage iron vs diamond tools — conserve diamonds for critical moments
F-air spike off the ledge for edgeguards
Block ladder combos: place block mid-air, jump off it, continue juggle
Elytra (up-B) stall tactics off-stage to bait and punish
Resource denial: mine aggressively to deny opponent stage positioning
Extended punish combos from grab using blocks for wall setups
TNT setup reads based on opponent's recovery trajectory
Optimal mining routes during neutral without giving up stage control
Block ladder kill setups: precise height and timing for maximum damage
Full edgeguard flowchart with Minecart, TNT, and Elytra coverage
Tool management optimization: when to spend iron vs save for diamonds
Exploiting Steve's wall creation to force 50/50 mix-ups at ledge

Snake.overview: Zoner with insane damage output. Grenades require careful management or they backfire.

skills: Cook grenades (neutral-B hold) before throwing for timed explosions
Nikita missile (side-B) can be guided — useful for ledge trapping
Up-tilt is one of the best anti-air kill moves in the game — use it often
C4 (down-B) on the ground as a trap or detonate it mid-combo
Up-smash (mortar) hits both sides and covers landings
Grenade camping: cook a grenade while an opponent approaches to force reactions
D-throw into N-air or Back-air combo at low percent
Detonate C4 mid-combo to extend punishes
Up-tilt kill confirms at high percent near top blastzones
Crawl under projectiles and low attacks using Snake's crawl
Grenade pull → jump → grenade toss to cover multiple angles at once
Back-air off stage for edgeguarding — it's fast and covers wide area
Cypher (up-B) stall to bait opponents off stage, then punish
Grenade cancel into grab or F-tilt for close-range mix-ups
Up-smash OOS (out of shield) for punishing laggy aerial approaches
Full grenade schedule: always have one cooked, one placed, and one ready
Ledge trap with Nikita + grenade + mortar for near-inescapable pressure
Optimal C4 placements for stage-specific kill setups
Adaptive gameplan: transition from zoning to punish game based on percent
Managing grenade self-damage and using it intentionally for rage activation
